notes: Windows + OpenCode + Ralph method (no upstream bash script). One iteration handles one story.

userStories.title: Implement feed CRUD API and file storage
userStories.description: Support creating, updating, deleting, and querying the feed list, and write to the data/feeds index.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests are written first and cover feed create/update/delete/query.
Deleting a feed deletes its corresponding directory.
API contract documentation is consistent with tests.

userStories.title: Implement RSS fetching and article persistence
userStories.description: Fetch articles per feed and provide queries in reverse chronological order, writing to data/feeds/{feedId}/articles.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover inserting new articles and idempotency for duplicates.
Article lists are sorted by published time in descending order.
Failures for one feed do not affect fetching for other feeds.

userStories.title: Implement summary profile management
userStories.description: Support configuring multiple AI summary profiles, including name/base_url/key/model/fields/prompt_template.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover creating, updating, deleting, and querying profiles.
The uniqueness constraint for profile names is clearly enforced.
The configuration storage structure is documented.

userStories.title: Implement manual triggering of article AI summaries
userStories.description: Allow users to select an article and a profile to trigger a summary, then write the result to a markdown file.
acceptanceCriteria: Templates support variables such as title and content.
Summary results are written to `.md` files.
Tests cover mocked AI calls and writing the output to disk.

userStories.title: Implement global cascading cleanup for profile delete/edit
userStories.description: When deleting or renaming a profile, remove all summary markdown and metadata with the same name under all feed articles.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover the full scope of global cleanup.
Summaries for other profiles are not accidentally deleted.
The cleanup process tolerates missing files.

userStories.title: Implement scheduled feed fetching
userStories.description: Run background tasks to fetch feeds at fixed intervals.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover the scheduling trigger logic.
Error isolation and logging are clear.
Manual triggers and scheduled tasks can coexist.

userStories.title: Implement basic frontend page flow
userStories.description: Build the page skeletons for feed management, article list, article summary, and summary configuration, and connect them to the APIs.
acceptanceCriteria: Routing and page structure are stable.
Key interactions are covered by tests.
Empty/loading/error states are visible.

userStories.title: Complete feed management interaction details
userStories.description: Refine feed management frontend behavior according to README user flow, including add/edit/delete/list refresh and user feedback states.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Users can add, edit, delete, and refresh feed list in one session, and UI state stays consistent after each operation.
Boundary/exception: Invalid URL, duplicate-like URL, and backend failure cases show explicit and actionable error feedback.
Regression: Existing feed list rendering and navigation entry points remain stable after interaction refinements.

userStories.title: Add UI automation for feed management flow
userStories.description: Create Windows-runnable UI automation coverage for feed management frontend flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path automation: End-to-end case verifies add/edit/delete/refresh feed flow and resulting list state changes.
Boundary/exception automation: Invalid input and mocked API failure cases assert visible validation/error feedback.
Regression automation: Existing feed management route load and baseline list rendering are covered to prevent breakage.
Windows execution: Test command, prerequisites, and target suite scope are documented.

userStories.title: Complete article list browsing interaction details
userStories.description: Refine article list frontend behavior by feed, including loading, empty state, refresh and failure-recovery interactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Users can enter feed article list, view loaded results, and refresh list without losing current context.
Boundary/exception: Empty list and fetch failure states are clearly visible, and retry behavior is available and stable.
Regression: Reverse chronological visibility and feed-to-list navigation behavior remain correct.

userStories.title: Add UI automation for article list flow
userStories.description: Create UI automation coverage for article list browsing flow and its key state transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path automation: Article list loading and refresh behavior is verified from feed entry to visible list rendering.
Boundary/exception automation: Empty-data and failed-fetch scenarios assert expected UI feedback and recovery actions.
Regression automation: Existing sort visibility and navigation behavior is covered with stable assertions.
Windows execution: Automation is runnable in local Windows workflow with documented command and scope.

userStories.title: Complete article summary page interaction details
userStories.description: Refine article summary frontend behavior for summary viewing and triggering operations with robust user feedback.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Users can select summary profile, trigger generation, and view generated summary result successfully.
Boundary/exception: No-profile-selected, no-data, and generation-failure states provide explicit user-visible feedback.
Regression: Repeated trigger attempts and page revisit keep summary-related UI behavior stable and predictable.

userStories.title: Add UI automation for article summary flow
userStories.description: Create UI automation coverage for article summary viewing and triggering frontend flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path automation: Profile selection and summary trigger flow validates in-progress and success UI feedback.
Boundary/exception automation: No-data and failed-generation scenarios assert expected message and interaction handling.
Regression automation: Existing summary view rendering and re-entry behavior are protected by automated checks.
Windows execution: Setup assumptions and run command are documented for local automation runs.

userStories.title: Complete summary profile management interaction details
userStories.description: Refine summary profile frontend behavior including create/edit/delete/list and validation/error feedback details.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Users can create, update, delete, and re-list summary profiles with consistent interaction feedback.
Boundary/exception: Required-field missing, name conflict, and backend failure cases are clearly handled in UI.
Regression: Existing profile list rendering and navigation behavior stays stable after interaction refinements.

userStories.title: Add UI automation for summary profile flow
userStories.description: Create UI automation coverage for summary profile management frontend flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path automation: Profile create/update/delete/list flow is covered with deterministic assertions.
Boundary/exception automation: Validation prompts, conflict errors, and API failure feedback are asserted.
Regression automation: Existing profile management page load and baseline list behavior remain protected.
Windows execution: Test suite command and prerequisites are documented in frontend test instructions.

userStories.title: Establish Tailwind UI foundation
userStories.description: Set up Tailwind styling foundation for frontend, including design tokens, layout primitives, and reusable style conventions.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Tailwind configuration, shared tokens, and base utility conventions are available and documented.
Boundary/exception: Legacy styles and Tailwind coexist without breaking current functional pages during transition.
Regression: Existing frontend behavior remains unchanged while foundational style layer is introduced.

userStories.title: Migrate core pages to Tailwind visual design
userStories.description: Apply Tailwind-based visual improvements to home, feed management, article list, article summary, and summary profile pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path: Core pages share consistent spacing, typography, and interaction styles after Tailwind migration.
Boundary/exception: Narrow-width layout and long-content scenarios remain readable and usable.
Regression: Page-level functionality and existing interaction paths stay stable after style migration.

userStories.title: Add UI verification for Tailwind visual regression
userStories.description: Add automated UI verification to guard against major visual regressions after Tailwind migration.
acceptanceCriteria: Happy path verification: Critical pages and key UI states have automated visual/UI checks with stable baselines.
Boundary/exception verification: Responsive breakpoints and error/empty/loading states are included in verification scope.
Regression verification: Baseline update process is documented and prevents accidental acceptance of unexpected UI drift.
Windows execution: Verification command and integration point with frontend quality gate are documented.
